description: Connect to NetSuite via SuiteQL and REST APIs. Query customers, invoices, vendors, transactions, and more using Token-Based Authentication (TBA).

credential_fields.label: Consumer Key
credential_fields.help: From NetSuite: Setup > Integration > Manage Integrations

credential_fields.label: Consumer Secret
credential_fields.help: Shown once when integration record is created

credential_fields.label: Token ID
credential_fields.help: From NetSuite: Setup > Users/Roles > Access Tokens

credential_fields.label: Token Secret
credential_fields.help: Shown once when access token is created

instance_id_help: Your NetSuite Account ID. Found in Setup > Company > Company Information. For sandbox, append _SB1.

additional_instance_fields.label: Account ID
additional_instance_fields.help: Your NetSuite Account ID. Found in Setup > Company > Company Information.

operations.name: Run SuiteQL Query
operations.description: Execute a SuiteQL query to retrieve data from any NetSuite record type. SuiteQL is a SQL-like language for querying NetSuite data.

parameters.name: q
parameters.label: SuiteQL Query
parameters.placeholder: SELECT id, companyname, email FROM customer WHERE isinactive = 'F'

parameters.name: limit
parameters.label: Result Limit
parameters.description: Maximum number of records to return (max 1000)

parameters.name: offset
parameters.label: Result Offset
parameters.description: Number of records to skip for pagination

body_template.q: SELECT id, entityid, companyname, email, phone, datecreated, lastmodifieddate, balance FROM customer WHERE isinactive = 'F' ORDER BY id

operations.name: Get Customer by ID
operations.description: Retrieve a single customer record by internal ID

parameters.name: customer_id
parameters.label: Customer Internal ID
parameters.description: The NetSuite internal ID of the customer

operations.name: Get Invoices
operations.description: Retrieve invoice records via SuiteQL with optional filters

parameters.name: date_from
parameters.label: Date From
parameters.description: Filter invoices on or after this date (YYYY-MM-DD)

body_template.q: SELECT t.id, t.tranid, t.trandate, t.entity, c.companyname, t.foreigntotal, t.status, t.duedate, t.memo FROM transaction t JOIN customer c ON t.entity = c.id WHERE t.type = 'CustInvc' ORDER BY t.trandate DESC

operations.name: Get Invoice by ID
operations.description: Retrieve a single invoice by internal ID

parameters.name: invoice_id
parameters.label: Invoice Internal ID
parameters.description: The NetSuite internal ID of the invoice

body_template.q: SELECT id, entityid, companyname, email, phone, balance, datecreated FROM vendor WHERE isinactive = 'F' ORDER BY id

operations.name: Get Sales Orders
operations.description: Retrieve sales order records via SuiteQL

body_template.q: SELECT t.id, t.tranid, t.trandate, t.entity, c.companyname, t.foreigntotal, t.status, t.memo FROM transaction t JOIN customer c ON t.entity = c.id WHERE t.type = 'SalesOrd' ORDER BY t.trandate DESC

operations.name: Get Purchase Orders
operations.description: Retrieve purchase order records via SuiteQL

body_template.q: SELECT t.id, t.tranid, t.trandate, t.entity, v.companyname, t.foreigntotal, t.status, t.memo FROM transaction t JOIN vendor v ON t.entity = v.id WHERE t.type = 'PurchOrd' ORDER BY t.trandate DESC

operations.name: Get Items
operations.description: Retrieve inventory and non-inventory item records

body_template.q: SELECT id, itemid, displayname, itemtype, salesdescription, baseprice, isinactive FROM item WHERE isinactive = 'F' ORDER BY itemid
SELECT id, acctnumber, acctname, accttype, balance, isinactive FROM account WHERE isinactive = 'F' ORDER BY acctnumber
SELECT id, entityid, firstname, lastname, email, title, department, isinactive FROM employee WHERE isinactive = 'F' ORDER BY lastname, firstname

setup_instructions:
## NetSuite Token-Based Authentication Setup

### Prerequisites
- NetSuite administrator access
- SuiteQL and REST Web Services features enabled

### Step 1: Enable Features
1. Go to **Setup > Company > Enable Features**
2. Under the **SuiteCloud** tab, enable:
   - **Token-Based Authentication**
   - **REST Web Services**
   - **SuiteQL**

### Step 2: Create an Integration Record
1. Go to **Setup > Integration > Manage Integrations > New**
2. Give it a name (e.g., "AI Hub")
3. Under **Authentication**, check **Token-Based Authentication**
4. Save. **Copy the Consumer Key and Consumer Secret** (shown only once)

### Step 3: Create an Access Token
1. Go to **Setup > Users/Roles > Access Tokens > New**
2. Select the **Application** (integration you just created)
3. Select the **User** and **Role** (must have REST and SuiteQL permissions)
4. Save. **Copy the Token ID and Token Secret** (shown only once)

### Step 4: Find Your Account ID
1. Go to **Setup > Company > Company Information**
2. Your **Account ID** is displayed at the top
3. For sandbox accounts, append _SB1 (e.g., 1234567_SB1)

### Step 5: Configure in AI Hub
1. Enter your Account ID
2. Enter Consumer Key and Consumer Secret
3. Enter Token ID and Token Secret
4. Test the connection

Need help? See the [NetSuite TBA Guide](https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/netsuite/ns-online-help/section_4247337262.html).
